intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: A voice-first or chat-like interface for logging health data feels more personal and less clinical than traditional form inputs
facade.label: Conversational checkins
facade.acceptImplies: User responds well to natural language interaction, prefers warmth over formality in data entry
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers structured, efficient input methods and finds chat-style logging inefficient or unprofessional for health tracking

evidence.hypothesis: A voice-first or chat-like interface for logging health data feels more personal and less clinical than traditional form inputs
evidence.implication: User responds well to natural language interaction, prefers warmth over formality in data entry

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer scannable summary cards for long-term experiments over dense monthly tables
facade.label: Monthly overview cards
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use card-based layouts with aggregated stats (e.g., adherence %, trend direction, one key number per experiment)
facade.rejectImplies: Users want detailed breakdowns and full data visibility in table format, not simplified summaries

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer animated panel transitions over instant appearance when viewing experiment results
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: The dashboard should use fluid motion to reveal data panels, charts, and metric updates rather than static pops or direct swaps
facade.rejectImplies: The dashboard should prioritize snappy, instant-load interactions with minimal animation to reduce perceived latency and cognitive load

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left navigation combined with card-based layouts reduces cognitive load for comparing experiment metrics over time
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. tab-switching; card-based vs. list-based content organization
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to experiment sections and visual grouping of related data; card metaphor supports scanning multiple metrics
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers immersive focus on single experiments, tabbed workflows, or dense lists for rapid data entry and comparison

facade.hypothesis: A health dashboard with gentle, curved UI elements creates a more approachable and less clinical feel than sharp corners, making daily health tracking feel supportive rather than sterile.
facade.axisTargeted: Geometric warmth — rounded vs. sharp edges
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers welcoming, organic shapes; likely favors curved cards, buttons, and containers; suggests a softer, more human-centered aesthetic.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clean, modern minimalism with defined edges; suggests they may value clarity and precision in the interface over emotional warmth; points toward a more utilitarian look.

facade.hypothesis: A personal health dashboard benefits from a calm, low-stimulation visual palette that feels medical-professional yet approachable—dark backgrounds with muted tones reduce eye strain during frequent check-ins and create introspective mood for self-reflection
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool + calm vs energetic
facade.label: Dark, muted colors
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward serious, restful aesthetics; probe next with textures (paper, soft shadows) and intimate scale to test how deep the 'calm' preference runs
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers brighter, more energetic mood; test vibrant accent colors and high-contrast interfaces that feel motivational and forward-moving rather than contemplative

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights about patterns
Whether user wants instant feedback and animations vs. static, stable information display

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A warm, encouraging tone with simple language in status updates makes health tracking feel supportive rather than clinical or judgmental
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality: warm/personal vs. formal/clinical
facade.label: Friendly daily check-in
facade.acceptImplies: User values encouragement and conversational warmth in feedback loops; may respond well to celebratory language, emoji, or gentle prompts
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, professional tone; sees warmth as potentially patronizing or distracting from data accuracy and clarity
